questions.text: A reliable measure of the hotness of an object is its:
questions.explanation: Temperature is the degree of hotness or coldness of an object.

questions.text: The range of a clinical thermometer is:
questions.explanation: Clinical thermometers are designed to measure human body temperature, which stays within this range.

questions.text: The process by which heat is transferred in solids is:
questions.explanation: Conduction is the transfer of heat from the hotter end to the colder end of an object without moving the object itself.

questions.text: Normal human body temperature is approximately:
questions.explanation: The average temperature of a healthy human body is 37° Celsius.

questions.text: Which of these is a good conductor of heat?
questions.explanation: Metals like copper and aluminum allow heat to pass through them easily.

questions.text: Heat from the Sun reaches us through the process of:
questions.explanation: Radiation does not require a medium (like air or water) to transfer heat.
During the day, land heats up faster than water, causing cool air from the sea to blow towards the land.
Dark surfaces absorb more heat, helping to keep the body warm in winter.

questions.text: The range of a laboratory thermometer is usually:
questions.explanation: Laboratory thermometers are designed for scientific experiments and have a wider range than clinical ones.

questions.text: A wooden spoon is dipped in a cup of ice cream. Its other end:
questions.explanation: Wood is an insulator (poor conductor) of heat, so heat does not flow through it.
